LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: JUDGE MILLER DISCLOSED THAT SHE KNOWS SIGRID IRIAS; THEY ARE BOTH MEMBERS OF THE SAME INN OF COURT. THIS FACT DOES NOT AFFECT THE COURT'S ABILITY TO BE FAIR AND IMPARTIAL IN THIS CASE. COURT GAVE COUNSEL THE OPPORTUNITY TO ASK QUESTIONS. NO QUESTIONS WERE ASKED OR OBJECTIONS WERE RAISED. THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS ROMAN CATHOLIC BISHOP OF SAN JOSE, ST. FRANCES CABRINI CHURCH & ST. FRANCES CABRINI SCHOOL'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE PUBLIC NUISANCE CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF HAS NO STANDING TO ASSERT HIS CLAIM AS HE IS AN ADULT. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ROMAN CATHOLIC ARCHBISHOP OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIRST, THIRD, NINTH, AND TENTH CAUSES OF ACTION. AS THE SUBSEQUENT EMPLOYER OF THE ALLEGED PERPETRATOR, THE ARCHBISHOP OF SAN FRANCISCO HAD NO DUTY TO PREVENT THE HARM ALLEGEDLY SUFFERED BY PLAINTIFF. THE PUBLIC NUISANCE CLAIMS FAILS AS A MATTER OF LAW. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077 =(302/HEK)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS FILED A 170.6 CHALLENGE TO JUDGE GOLDSMITH. THEREFORE, THE TENTATIVE RULING IS VACATED AND DEFENDANTS DIOCESE OF FRESNO EDUCATION CORPORATION, THE ROMAN CATHOLIC BISHOP OF FRESNOS MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS CONTINUED TO JANUARY 23, 2013 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; NOT REPORTED.

DISCOVERY 302, A R G U E D; DEFENDANTS DIOCESE OF FRESNO EDUCATION CORPORATION, THE ROMAN CATHOLIC BISHOP OF FRESNO, A CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER IS DENIED. WHILE THE COURT IS SENSITIVE TO MONSIGNOR MINHOTO'S ('MINHOTO') HEALTH CONCERNS, THE COURT IS PERSUADED THAT THIS DEPOSITION SHOULD PROCEED SUBJECT TO THE LIMITATIONS OFFERED BY PLAINTIFF'S COUNSELI.E., THE DEPOSITION WILL BE LIMITED TO ONE HOUR, IT WILL TAKE PLACE AT MINHOTO'S ASSISTED LIVING FACILITY OR WHATEVER OTHER LOCATION IS MOST CONVENIENT FOR HIM, MINHOTO WILL BE PERMITTED TO TAKE ANY BREAKS HE NEEDS DURING THE DEPOSITION, AND COUNSEL SHALL BE POLITE AND RESPECTFUL TO MINHOTO AT ALL TIMES DURING THE DEPOSITION. THE COURT IS PERSUADED THAT MINHOTO'S TESTIMONY IS POTENTIALLY PROBATIVE OF THE ISSUE OF DEFENDANT DIOCESE OF FRESNO EDUCATION CORPORATION'S KNOWLEDGE OF THE RISK ALLEGEDLY POSED BY FATHER FLICKINGER, AND DEFENDANTS MAKE NO ATTEMPT TO ARGUE TO THE CONTRARY. PLAINTIFF HAS ALSO ARGUEDAND DEFENDANTS DO NOT DISPUTE -- THAT MINHOTO'S TESTIMONY MAY HAVE ADDITIONAL RELEVANCE IN THAT HE LATER HAD INVOLVEMENT IN EMPLOYMENT DECISIONS REGARDING FATHER FLICKINGER AS CHANCELLOR. DR. TETRUD'S DECLARATION DOES NOT STATE WHETHER HIS CONCLUSIONS WOULD APPLY TO A DEPOSITION CONDUCTED UNDER THE LIMITATIONS OFFERED BY PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL, AND FAULTY MEMORY IS ALWAYS A CONCERN IN LITIGATION IN ANY CASE. AND THERE IS AT LEAST SOME SUGGESTION FROM MONSIGNOR MORETON AND DEFENSE COUNSEL THAT MINHOTO IS CAPABLE OF GIVING DEPOSITION TESTIMONY. FOR THE FOREGOING REASONS, THE COURT IS NOT PERSUADED THAT 'JUSTICE REQUIRES' AN ORDER THAT THIS DEPOSITION NOT BE TAKEN, NOR IS THE COURT PERSUADED THAT THE BURDENS CLEARLY OUTWEIGH THE BENEFITS SOUGHT TO BE OBTAINED FROM THE DEPOSITION. SEE CCP 2025.420(B) AND 2017.020(A). ACCORDINGLY, THE MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER IS DENIED, AND MINHOTO'S DEPOSITION SHALL PROCEED SUBJECT TO THE LIMITATIONS SET FORTH ABOVE. ORDER SIGNED. SEE ORDER FOR FULL TERMS.

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF MATTHEW FRAZER'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-11-515938, CGC-12-524810 WITH CASE CGC-11-513216 IS DENIED. THE CRITERIA OF CCP 1048(A) ARE NOT MET. THE CASES INVOLVE THREE DIFFERENT PLAINTIFFS WHO ALLEGE CHILD SEXUAL ABUSE ALLEGEDLY OCCURRING AT DIFFERENT TIMES (1992; 1996-2000; AND 2011, RESPECTIVELY), IN DIFFERENT WAYS, AND AT DIFFERENT LOCATIONS. JOINTLY TRYING THE CASES RAISES THE RISK OF CONFUSING THE JURY AND PREJUDICING DEFENDANTS. FURTHER, PLAINTIFFS' CASE AUTHORITY DOES NOT SUPPORT CONSOLIDATION IN THIS CASE. SEE, E.G., TODD-STENBERG V. DALKON SHIELD CLAIMANT TRUST (1996) 48 CAL. APP. 4TH 976 (CONSOLIDATION OF MULTIPLE PLAINTIFFS SEEKING PERSONAL INJURIES FROM THE SAME MEDICAL DEVICE) (CITED BY PLAINTIFFS). IN ADDITION, THE THREE CASES ARE IN DIFFERENT STAGES; MORE THAN 25 DEPOSITIONS HAVE BEEN TAKEN IN THE FRAZER CASE AND IT IS SET FOR TRIAL ON SEPTEMBER 30, 2013. THE OTHER TWO CASES ARE NOT AT ISSUE AND DISCOVERY IS JUST BEGINNING. CONSOLIDATION WOULD DELAY THE FRAZER TRIAL. THE MOTION FOR TRIAL CONTINUANCE IN THE FRAZER CASE CONTINGENT ON THE REQUEST FOR CONSOLIDATION IS THUS DENIED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO CSR7489.

LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS ROMAN CATHOLIC BISHOP OF SAN JOSE, ST. FRANCIS CABRINI CHURCH AND ST FRANCIS CABRINI SCHOOL'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS CONTINUED TO JANUARY 28, 2013 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302 ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: JUDGE MILLER DISCLOSED THAT SHE KNOWS SIGRID IRIAS; THEY ARE BOTH MEMBERS OF THE SAME INN OF COURT. THIS FACT DOES NOT AFFECT THE COURT'S ABILITY TO BE FAIR AND IMPARTIAL IN THIS CASE. COURT GAVE COUNSEL THE OPPORTUNITY TO ASK QUESTIONS. NO QUESTIONS WERE ASKED OR OBJECTIONS WERE RAISED. THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS DIOCESE OF FRESNO EDUCATION CORPORATION & THE ROMAN CATHOLIC BISHOP OF FRESNO'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE PUBLIC NUISANCE CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF HAS NO STANDING TO ASSERT HIS CLAIM AS HE IS AN ADULT. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-11-515938, CGC-12-524810 WITH CASE CGC-11-513216
Off Calendar Aug-05-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF MATTHEW FRAZER'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-11-515938, CGC-12-524810 WITH CASE CGC-11-513216 is denied. The criteria of CCP 1048(a) are not met. The cases involve three different plaintiffs who allege child sexual abuse allegedly occurring at different times (1992; 1996-2000; and 2011, respectively), in different ways, and at different locations. Jointly trying the cases raises the risk of confusing the jury and prejudicing defendants. Further, Plaintiffs' case authority does not support consolidation in this case. See, e.g., Todd-Stenberg v. Dalkon Shield Claimant Trust (1996) 48 Cal. App. 4th 976 (consolidation of multiple plaintiffs seeking personal injuries from the same medical device) (cited by plaintiffs). In addition, the three cases are in different stages; more than 25 depositions have been taken in the Frazer case and it is set for trial on September 30, 2013. The other two cases are not at issue and discovery is just beginning. Consolidation would delay the Frazer trial. The motion for trial continuance in the Frazer case contingent on the request for consolidation is thus denied. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Melanie Gheno CSR7489.

Defendants Diocese Of Fresno Education Corporation, The Roman Catholic Bishop Of Fresno, A Corporation'S Motion For Protective Order
Off Calendar Jan-14-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Timothy C. Hale, Nye Peabody Stirling & Hale, 805.963.2345, appearing for the Plaintiff;
Michelle Pepper, Stammer McKnight Barnum & Bailey, 559.449.0571, appearing for Defendant Roman Catholic Biship of Freson.

Ruling: A R G U E D; Defendants Diocese Of Fresno Education Corporation, The Roman Catholic Bishop Of Fresno, A Corporation'S Motion For Protective Order is DENIED. While the Court is sensitive to Monsignor Minhoto's ('Minhoto') health concerns, the Court is persuaded that this deposition should proceed subject to the limitations offered by Plaintiff's counsel ' i.e., the deposition will be limited to one hour, it will take place at Minhoto's assisted living facility or whatever other location is most convenient for him, Minhoto will be permitted to take any breaks he needs during the deposition, and counsel shall be polite and respectful to Minhoto at all times during the deposition. The Court is persuaded that Minhoto's testimony is potentially probative of the issue of defendant Diocese of Fresno Education Corporation's knowledge of the risk allegedly posed by Father Flickinger, and Defendants make no attempt to argue to the contrary. Plaintiff has also argued ' and Defendants do not dispute -- that Minhoto's testimony may have additional relevance in that he later had involvement in employment decisions regarding Father Flickinger as Chancellor. Dr. Tetrud's declaration does not state whether his conclusions would apply to a deposition conducted under the limitations offered by Plaintiff's counsel, and faulty memory is always a concern in litigation in any case. And there is at least some suggestion from Monsignor Moreton and Defense counsel that Minhoto is capable of giving deposition testimony. For the foregoing reasons, the Court is not persuaded that 'justice requires' an order that this deposition not be taken, nor is the Court persuaded that the burdens clearly outweigh the benefits sought to be obtained from the deposition. See CCP 2025.420(b) and 2017.020(a). Accordingly, the motion for protective order is denied, and Minhoto's deposition shall proceed subject to the limitations set forth above. Order signed. See order for full terms. Pro Tem Judge: Anthony Schoenberg, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Not reported. =302/JPT

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-02-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. TIMOTHY C. HALE OF THE NYE, PEABODY & HALE LLP, TEL. NO. (805) 963-2345 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. DANIEL C. ZAMORA & ATTY. PAUL E. GASPARI OF THE WEINTRAUB LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 772-9639 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT ROMAN CATHOLIC ARCHBISHOP OF SAN FRANCISCO;
ATTY. KURT J. SEIBERT, TEL. NO. (408) 332-5838 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT FLICKINGER.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANT ROMAN CATHOLIC ARCHBISHOP OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS Sustained without leave to amend as to the first, third, ninth, and tenth causes of action. As the subsequent employer of the alleged perpetrator, the Archbishop of San Francisco had no duty to prevent the harm allegedly suffered by plaintiff. The public nuisance claims fails as a matter of law. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077 =(302/HEK)
